—JOHN SCHNEIDER
**I** n the 1990s, I was a teacher at a small private school in New York City. I had a student named Jake. Jake was a quiet, shy boy who had a hard time making friends. He was a good student, but he was also a bit of a loner.
One day, I was walking down the hallway and I heard a commotion coming from Jake's classroom. I walked in and saw Jake sitting at his desk, surrounded by his classmates. They were all laughing and talking excitedly.
I asked Jake what was going on, and he told me that his classmates had been talking about how they were going to play a prank on him. They had decided to put a note on his desk that said, "You are the worst student in the world."
I was surprised by this, but I also knew that Jake was a good kid and that he didn't deserve to be treated this way. So I decided to play a prank on them.
